components.title: Access Control Policy
components.description: Defines the organization's requirements for managing user accounts, access rights, separation of duties, and least privilege across all information systems. Establishes processes for account provisioning, periodic access review, and de-provisioning.
components.purpose: Establish and enforce access control requirements to protect organizational assets.

control-implementations.description: Control implementations for Access Control Policy.

implemented-requirements.description: This policy documents the access control policy and procedures required by AC-1. It defines roles, responsibilities, review frequency, and the authority for granting and revoking access to organizational systems.

This policy defines the account management lifecycle: account types permitted, authorization requirements for account creation, conditions for disabling and removing accounts, and the quarterly access review process for all system accounts.
This policy requires that access to systems and data is enforced in accordance with approved authorizations, role-based access control (RBAC) assignments, and the principle of least privilege.

This policy mandates separation of duties for sensitive functions including financial transactions, system administration, and security monitoring. Defines roles that must not be combined in a single user account.

This policy requires least privilege access: users are granted only the minimum permissions required to perform their job functions. Privileged access requires documented justification and management approval.
This policy defines requirements for remote access including mandatory MFA, encrypted tunnels (VPN), session timeout, and approval requirements for remote access to organizational systems.

This policy governs the use of external systems and personally-owned devices to access organizational information. Defines permitted use cases and required security controls for bring-your-own-device (BYOD) scenarios.
